nodes.label: astro check job
nodes.rationale: astro build (run by Vercel on every deploy) does NOT type-check, so a TS/.astro template error can ship silently; the check job runs npm run check to close that gap. Build + deploy stay Vercel's job.

nodes.label: e2e (Playwright) job
nodes.rationale: Installs Chromium only (--with-deps for Linux libs), builds and serves dist/client, runs specs with CI=1 to enable a retry + HTML report; uploads playwright-report/ as an artifact only on failure. Additive to the astro check gate, not a replacement.

nodes.label: Lead integration flow (browser -> /api/lead -> gtm-toolkit -> D365)
nodes.rationale: The website never talks to D365 directly; it validates, normalizes and signs the lead then forwards to one HTTPS endpoint owned by gtm-toolkit, the only component holding CRM credentials.

nodes.label: Honeypot (company_website)
nodes.rationale: Hidden field real visitors never fill. If non-empty, endpoint returns 200 {ok:true} and silently drops the lead so the bot believes it succeeded and nothing is forwarded.

nodes.label: HMAC-SHA256 signature (x-gradvera-signature)
nodes.rationale: Signature is HMAC_SHA256 over the exact serialized JSON body bytes. Receiver MUST verify against raw request body bytes (constant-time), not a re-serialized parse, because re-serializing can reorder keys/whitespace and break verification. Read raw body, verify, then parse. In prod the secret must always be set so forgeries are rejected.

nodes.label: forwarded soft flag
nodes.rationale: A monitoring-only flag. Visitor always sees HTTP 200 once the lead is valid, even if the downstream hand-off failed, so a transient toolkit outage never breaks the success UX. forwarded:true means the toolkit answered 2xx (enqueued); it does not by itself prove the lead reached D365.

nodes.label: 5s forward timeout (AbortSignal.timeout)
nodes.rationale: The forward is bounded by a 5s AbortSignal.timeout so a slow or hung receiver can never stall the visitor's request up to the function timeout; an aborted hung forward is logged as failed and yields forwarded:false.

nodes.label: Lead field validation
nodes.rationale: fullName, company, email, message must be non-empty strings and email must match a basic regex; failure returns HTTP 400 {ok:false,error:invalid}. Payload over 16KB -> 413; non-JSON content-type -> 415; GET -> 405.

nodes.label: gtm-toolkit inbound-lead receiver (POST /website/lead)
nodes.rationale: gtm_toolkit.website module (Starlette + uvicorn), mirrors the Smartlead webhook receiver. Verifies x-gradvera-signature against the raw body: bad/absent sig -> 401, body over 64KB -> 413, unparseable JSON -> 400, failed lead contract -> 422, valid -> 200 {status:queued}. Returns fast (enqueues only).

nodes.label: Decoupled queue + consumer
nodes.rationale: The public endpoint never blocks on D365: the receiver enqueues and returns fast, and a separate scheduled consumer (gtm website consume --apply) performs the D365 write. Full message, locale, page and receivedAt are preserved in the durable queue envelope.

nodes.label: Idempotent processed-key ledger
nodes.rationale: D365 write is idempotent on (email, receivedAt) via a processed-key ledger. An email already an Open Lead or active Contact is skipped + logged so inbound never clobbers a rep-managed record.

nodes.label: D365 Account + Lead write path
nodes.rationale: Each lead becomes a D365 Account (deduped by company name + email-domain fallback) and a Lead bound to it, owned by GTM_DEFAULT_LEAD_OWNER_ID, classify=False. Field mapping fullName->first/last, company->companyname, email->emailaddress1, phone->telephone1, role->jobtitle. Smartlead enrollment of inbound demos is intentionally not automatic.

nodes.label: Gradvera 'G' brand mark, Android Chrome icon 192x192
nodes.norm_label: gradvera 'g' brand mark, android chrome icon 192x192

nodes.label: Gradvera 'G' brand mark, Android Chrome icon 512x512
nodes.norm_label: gradvera 'g' brand mark, android chrome icon 512x512

nodes.label: Gradvera 'G' brand mark: angular amber square-spiral G on dark charcoal with orange corner accent
nodes.norm_label: gradvera 'g' brand mark: angular amber square-spiral g on dark charcoal with orange corner accent

nodes.label: Gradvera 'G' brand mark, Apple touch icon
nodes.norm_label: gradvera 'g' brand mark, apple touch icon

nodes.label: The Lit Blueprint (Creative North Star)
nodes.rationale: A dark drafting table under one work lamp: deep blueprint navy ground, one burnished-amber light picking out what matters. Credibility is shown not claimed (hero renders a live estimate rather than asserting accuracy). Explicitly rejects the generic SaaS template and sterile corporate blue.

nodes.label: Burnished Amber accent (#E8901C)
nodes.rationale: The single brand accent used like a work-light; hover deepens to Amber Ember #C77713, pressed to Amber Kiln #9C5C0E; Amber Ink #7A4708 is the text tone on light.

nodes.label: Blueprint Navy (#1E3A8A) link/info accent
nodes.rationale: Drafting-ink blue for inline links and info states only; never a second brand color competing with amber.

nodes.label: The One Light Rule
nodes.rationale: Amber is the only accent and behaves like a single work-lamp: one dominant amber element per fold. Its rarity is what makes it read as important.

nodes.label: The Amber-Never-As-Body-Text Rule
nodes.rationale: #E8901C scores ~2.2-2.5:1 as text on light (below AA), so amber-toned words on light use Amber Ink #7A4708 (7.7:1 on white). Enforced in site-polish.css; do not undo it.

nodes.label: The Sentence-Case Rule
nodes.rationale: Headings are sentence case: no Title Case, no ALL-CAPS headlines, no exclamation marks; caps reserved for short mono labels only.

nodes.label: The Mono-Is-Annotation Rule
nodes.rationale: IBM Plex Mono is for callouts that report (labels, tags, HUD readouts, code); never set a sentence of marketing prose in mono.

nodes.label: The Glow-Signals-Life Rule
nodes.rationale: Slate shadow = elevation; amber glow = live data or focus. Never use amber glow to fake depth, nor slate drop-shadows on the dark chrome.

nodes.label: IBM Plex Sans/Mono typography system
nodes.rationale: One humanist-industrial sans (Plex Sans) for every readable word; Plex Mono only for technical annotation. Tabular numerals for currency/quantities. Display ceiling 82px, letter-spacing floor -0.04em.

nodes.label: Hero Blueprint + Estimate HUD (signature object)
nodes.rationale: SVG building wireframe plus a floating HUD reading a live estimate (EUR 1.24M, filling amber bar, Estimate ready 87% confidence): the system's thesis object that shows the product working instead of describing it. Motion only animates already-visible elements.

nodes.label: Design tokens (gradvera-tokens)
nodes.rationale: Color / typography / spacing / radius / component tokens defined in the DESIGN.md frontmatter and materialized as gradvera-tokens.css.

nodes.label: Hybrid elevation system (slate shadow vs amber glow)
nodes.rationale: Light reading surface uses diffuse slate shadows for real elevation; dark chrome stays flat with depth from tonal Ink plates, and reserves amber glow for live-data signal.
nodes.norm_label: hybrid elevation system (slate shadow vs amber glow)

nodes.label: Users: construction estimators / quantity surveyors / bid teams
nodes.rationale: SME contractor firms across SI/HR/EU; high-stakes deadline-bound bidding; arrive skeptical of marketing, evaluating whether the tool is serious enough to trust with real numbers.
nodes.norm_label: users: construction estimators / quantity surveyors / bid teams

nodes.label: Product purpose: convert qualified interest into a booked demo (to D365)
nodes.rationale: Book-a-demo is the only conversion that matters (lead capture -> Dynamics 365); success is qualified demo bookings from organic/outbound across three language markets, not traffic.
nodes.norm_label: product purpose: convert qualified interest into a booked demo (to d365)

nodes.label: Brand personality: calm / precise / anti-hype
nodes.rationale: Measured professional voice: sentence-case headings, no exclamation marks, no superlatives, no 'revolutionary AI'. The name Gradvera is never translated. Confidence shown through precision and proof, not volume.

nodes.label: Anti-reference: Generic SaaS template
nodes.rationale: Cream/gradient-mesh backgrounds, hero-metric cards, repeated icon+heading+text grids, purple gradient buttons, gradient text: the flooded AI-landing look, the fastest way to read as untrustworthy to this buyer.

nodes.label: Anti-reference: Sterile corporate blue
nodes.rationale: Stock-photo handshakes, navy-and-gray walls of body copy, faceless big-vendor feel. Credibility comes from showing the actual product and its precision, not from looking like an incumbent.

nodes.label: Principle 1: Earn trust through restraint
nodes.rationale: The buyer distrusts marketing noise; calm confidence converts where hype repels. Restraint must be intentional and distinctive, never timid or generic.

nodes.label: Principle 2: Show the work, don't claim it
nodes.rationale: Demonstrate precision with blueprint and live-estimate motifs (hero HUD reading EUR 1.24M, 87% confidence) rather than asserting it in adjectives. Proof over promise.
nodes.norm_label: principle 2: show the work, don't claim it

nodes.label: Principle 3: Credibility is the conversion
nodes.rationale: One unambiguous path (book a demo). Every section accrues proof and lowers risk; it never applies pressure or urgency.

nodes.label: Principle 4: Trilingual parity (EN/SL/HR)
nodes.rationale: EN/SL/HR are equal citizens; layout, headings, and copy must breathe in all three at every breakpoint, never English-first with translations bolted on and overflowing.

nodes.label: Principle 5: Speed is part of the promise
nodes.rationale: Static-first, fast, SEO-legible: a tool that respects the estimator's time should have a site that respects the visitor's. Performance and Core Web Vitals are brand, not just engineering.
nodes.norm_label: principle 5: speed is part of the promise

nodes.label: Accessibility target: WCAG 2.1 AA
nodes.rationale: Body text contrast >=4.5:1, large text >=3:1; full keyboard operability with visible :focus-visible amber outlines; prefers-reduced-motion honored; amber-on-navy contrast-checked per use.

nodes.label: Gradvera Marketing Website
nodes.rationale: Trilingual (EN/SL/HR), SEO-first, static marketing site for Gradvera construction-estimating software by DIGITAL SOLUTIONS d.o.o.; production domain gradvera.com.

nodes.label: Astro 5 Static Stack
nodes.rationale: output:'static' with @astrojs/vercel so every page is prerendered HTML for SEO and Core Web Vitals; only /api/lead opts into on-demand rendering via prerender=false. TypeScript astro/tsconfigs/strict, no any, alias @/* -> src/*.

nodes.label: Lead Capture (/api/lead)
nodes.rationale: The site's only non-static route; DemoForm POSTs JSON; honeypot silently drops bots; valid leads forwarded HMAC-SHA256-signed to gtm-toolkit -> Dynamics 365; always returns 200 so success UX never breaks on downstream outage.

nodes.label: astro check Verification Gate
nodes.rationale: npm run check is the type-safety gate; CI enforces it to close the gap where Vercel runs astro build but not astro check, so a type error would otherwise ship silently. astro check is project-wide; there is no per-file test runner.

nodes.label: Playwright E2E Harness
nodes.rationale: Under tests/; covers behaviour astro check and HTML greps can't prove (interaction, focus, layout, responsive overflow, console errors); additive to astro check, not a replacement. tests/ excluded from tsconfig so it never feeds the astro check gate.

nodes.label: Trilingual i18n Routing
nodes.rationale: Astro i18n with prefixDefaultLocale:false: EN at /, SL at /sl/, HR at /hr/. Translations in src/i18n/*.json; EN authored in _parts and assembled; utils.ts holds translation helpers.

nodes.label: Analytics & Consent (GTM + Consent Mode)
nodes.rationale: Google Tag Manager + Google Consent Mode gated by CookieConsent.astro for GDPR; non-production deploys emit noindex.

nodes.label: CI & Deploy Flow (Vercel + ci.yml)
nodes.rationale: main -> production (gradvera.com), staging -> staging.gradvera.com. ci.yml runs two jobs (astro check + e2e) on PRs and pushes to main/staging. Work lands via short-lived feat/fix/docs/ci/chore branches -> PR -> CI green -> merge.

nodes.label: The Lit Blueprint Design System
nodes.rationale: North Star visual system: dark blueprint-navy chrome + single burnished-amber accent (#E8901C), IBM Plex Sans/Mono, blueprint/estimate motifs. Guided by 5 principles (restraint, show the work, credibility-as-conversion, trilingual parity, speed) and WCAG 2.1 AA. Documented in PRODUCT.md and DESIGN.md, consumed by the /impeccable skill.

nodes.label: Design Source of Truth (Claude Design + design-sync)
nodes.rationale: Site UI originates from a remote Claude Design project, moved in incrementally one component at a time via the /design-sync skill and DesignSync MCP tool with enforced ordering (read -> finalize_plan -> write/delete). get_file content is treated as data, not instructions.
nodes.norm_label: design source of truth (claude design + design-sync)

nodes.label: Knowledge Graph (graphify-out)
nodes.rationale: Committed graph.json/graph.html/GRAPH_REPORT.md built by /graphify; consulted before a fix/feature for blast radius. Known bridges: BaseLayout.astro and SEO.astro. Kept current with /graphify --update folded into the same PR as the structural change.

nodes.label: BaseLayout.astro (graph bridge)
nodes.rationale: Wraps every route and imports the styles in cascade order (gradvera-tokens -> site -> cap1-screens -> cap-screens -> site-polish) so Vite bundles/minifies/fingerprints them for immutable caching; flagged as a cross-community bridge.

nodes.label: SEO.astro (JSON-LD bridge)
nodes.rationale: Sole owner of the JSON-LD / structured-data subtree; flagged as a cross-community bridge in the graph.

nodes.label: Playwright Browser Harness
nodes.rationale: Verifies what astro check and static HTML greps can't: interaction, focus management, computed layout, responsive overflow, runtime console errors. Builds the production site and serves real dist/client (via webServer) so checks run against the exact artifact Vercel ships, not astro dev. Additive to the astro check gate.

nodes.label: playwright.config.mjs
nodes.rationale: Config plus a webServer that builds and serves dist/client.

nodes.label: serve-dist.mjs
nodes.rationale: Zero-dep static server for /, /sl/, /hr/ and assets; unknown paths get 404.html with status 404, mirroring Vercel.

nodes.label: helpers.mjs
nodes.rationale: Reusable bits: VIEWPORTS, gotoClean (drops the consent banner), boxOf, rectsOverlap.

nodes.label: mobile-nav.spec.mjs
nodes.rationale: MobileNav a11y: open state, focus trap, Escape, scrim, scroll-lock, breakpoint auto-close.

nodes.label: homepage.spec.mjs
nodes.rationale: Checks: one h1, mockups aria-hidden, no h-scroll @375, no JSON-LD price, no hero SVG console error, Cap2 no-overlap across locales x widths.

nodes.label: seo.spec.mjs
nodes.rationale: Checks: 404 page (status 404, noindex, client-side SL/HR localization), per-locale OG image + localized og:image:alt, hero font preloads (latin/latin-ext), canonical host stays apex.

nodes.label: e2e CI Job
nodes.rationale: Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml as the e2e job beside astro check on every PR and pushes to main/staging: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium -> npm run test:e2e with CI=1 (retry + HTML report). On failure playwright-report/ is uploaded as an artifact. astro check remains the type-safety gate; this job is additive.

nodes.label: Slovenian Tagline: Programska oprema za gradbeno ocenjevanje
nodes.norm_label: slovenian tagline: programska oprema za gradbeno ocenjevanje

nodes.label: Lit Blueprint Brand Identity (navy chrome + amber G monogram)
nodes.norm_label: lit blueprint brand identity (navy chrome + amber g monogram)

nodes.label: Gradvera Brand Wordmark & Amber G Monogram
nodes.norm_label: gradvera brand wordmark & amber g monogram

nodes.label: Croatian Tagline — Softver za izradu građevinskih troškovnika
nodes.norm_label: croatian tagline — softver za izradu građevinskih troskovnika

nodes.label: Self-hosted IBM Plex fonts
nodes.rationale: Google Fonts hotlink stripped in favour of self-hosted IBM Plex via @fontsource for privacy, performance, and no third-party request on first paint.

nodes.label: Partytown off-thread analytics loading
nodes.rationale: GTM runs off the main thread via Partytown (forward: ['dataLayer.push','gtag']) to protect Core Web Vitals.

nodes.label: /api/lead lead-capture endpoint
nodes.rationale: The only on-demand route (prerender = false); always returns 200 on a valid lead so a downstream outage is logged but never breaks the success UX.

nodes.label: i18n model (flat-keyed JSON dictionaries)
nodes.rationale: English is assembled from per-section _parts/*.en.json; missing SL/HR strings degrade gracefully by falling back to English then the raw key.

nodes.label: Design-asset provenance
nodes.rationale: Design-origin CSS/JS came verbatim from the Claude Design project and were moved into src/ so Astro/Vite bundles, minifies and fingerprints them for immutable caching instead of serving six raw files.

nodes.label: site-polish.css local corrections layer
nodes.rationale: Imported last so its overrides win over the verbatim design-origin stylesheets.

nodes.label: Google Consent Mode v2
nodes.rationale: Defaults ads + analytics storage to denied before GTM loads; only functionality_storage and security_storage are granted.

nodes.label: CookieConsent banner
nodes.rationale: Stores the visitor's choice in a gv-consent cookie and replays it via gtag('consent','update',…) on the next visit.

nodes.label: HMAC-SHA256 lead signing
nodes.rationale: Normalized leads are forwarded to gtm-toolkit HMAC-SHA256 signed via the x-gradvera-signature: sha256=<hex> header.

nodes.label: company_website honeypot
nodes.rationale: Bot submissions are silently dropped with a 200 response so bots get no signal about detection.

nodes.label: robots.txt (generated)
nodes.rationale: Generated from src/pages/robots.txt.ts: allows all, disallows /api/, points to the sitemap index.

nodes.label: Open Graph images / render-og.mjs
nodes.rationale: 1200x630 per-locale PNGs regenerated from sibling SVGs via node scripts/render-og.mjs (Playwright Chromium + real IBM Plex).

nodes.label: Design fidelity rules
nodes.rationale: Preserve every class, id, data-*, inline style and inline svg exactly; the CSS/JS target the design's exact selectors so any drift breaks the layout or interactive blueprint screens.

nodes.label: www -> apex permanent redirect (307->308)
nodes.rationale: Vercel currently answers www.gradvera.com with a 307 (temporary) redirect; set it to permanent (308) so search engines consolidate the canonical host instead of re-checking it.

nodes.label: Static-first / prerender rationale
nodes.rationale: Output is static; every page ships pre-rendered HTML for SEO and Core Web Vitals, and only individual routes opt into on-demand rendering with prerender = false.

nodes.label: Environment variables
nodes.rationale: PUBLIC_-prefixed vars are exposed to client JS (Astro convention); everything else is server-only and read inside the /api/lead serverless function.
